paper_metadata.title: Global Context Vision Transformers
paper_metadata.abstract: We propose global context vision transformer (GC ViT), a novel architecture that enhances parameter and compute utilization for computer vision. Our method leverages global context self-attention modules, joint with standard local self-attention, to effectively and efficiently model both long and short-range spatial interactions, without the need for expensive operations such as computing attention masks or shifting local windows. In addition, we address the lack of the inductive bias in ViTs, and propose to leverage a modified fused inverted residual blocks in our architecture. Our proposed GC ViT achieves state-of-the-art results across image classification, object detection and semantic segmentation tasks. On ImageNet-1K dataset for classification, the variants of GC ViT with 51M, 90M and 201M parameters achieve 84.3%, 85.0% and 85.7% Top-1 accuracy, respectively, at 224 image resolution and without any pre-training, hence surpassing comparably-sized prior art such as CNN-based ConvNeXt and ViT-based MaxViT and Swin Transformer by a large margin. Pre-trained GC ViT backbones in downstream tasks of object detection, instance segmentation, and semantic segmentation using MS COCO and ADE20K datasets outperform prior work consistently. Specifically, GC ViT with a 4-scale DINO detection head achieves a box AP of 58.3 on MS COCO dataset.

extraction_data.innovation: Introduces a global query generator and global self-attention mechanism that enables long-range spatial context modeling by interacting global query tokens with local key and value representations within local windows, enhancing receptive field without increasing computational cost.
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Image classification, object detection, semantic segmentation
extraction_data.research_problem: Image classification, object detection, semantic segmentation
extraction_data.application: Image classification, object detection, semantic segmentation

extraction_data.innovation: Introduces global self-attention that enables modeling of long-range spatial dependencies by generating global query tokens from contextual features, which attend to local key-value regions without subsampling, enhancing performance on high-resolution tasks.
extraction_data.application: Image classification, object detection, instance segmentation, semantic segmentation
extraction_data.research_problem: Vision Transformer efficiency and long-range spatial modeling in computer vision

extraction_data.innovation: The key innovation is the Global Context Vision Transformer (GCViT) architecture, which incorporates a global query token to capture long-range dependencies across image patches, improving performance on vision tasks such as object detection and segmentation.

extraction_data.extension: Introduces a global query mechanism that enables long-range context modeling across image regions, enhancing performance in segmentation and detection tasks.
